title: bank of canada / canada / inflation | Data Release Other | Indicators of Capacity and Inflation Pressures for Canada
summary: The Bank of Canada publishes a comprehensive dashboard of capacity and inflation pressure indicators, updated one day after each rate announcement. Latest data shows Canadian CPI inflation at 1.8% year-over-year in February 2026, below the 2% target, with multiple measures of core inflation (CPI-trim 2.3%, CPI-median 2.3%, CPI-common 2.4%) also running near or slightly above target. The output gap is estimated between -0.8% and -1.6% depending on methodology, indicating below-potential economic activity. Labour market slack is evident with unemployment at 6.7% and wage growth decelerating across most measures to 2.6-3.9% year-over-year.
